description: Schema-driven corpus for iac-security. Misconfiguration intent (public storage, open ingress, privileged containers, hardcoded secrets) needs schema-aware judgement beyond a single regex, so the runner smoke-validates shape; fixtures double as ground truth for the LLM eval harness. detect = Terraform/k8s that violates a NEVER rule; ignore = the locked-down equivalent following ALWAYS.

fixtures.reason: bucket holding customer exports is public-read with all public-access-block protections disabled, exposing data to the internet (CWE-732 Incorrect Permission Assignment, CIS AWS S3 benchmark)
bucket has every public-access-block flag enabled and customer-managed KMS encryption at rest, keeping it private (follows ALWAYS: encrypt with CMK, block public access)
SSH (22) and PostgreSQL (5432) are open to 0.0.0.0/0, exposing admin and database ports to the entire internet (CWE-284 Improper Access Control; NEVER: open 22/5432 to 0.0.0.0/0)
database ingress is restricted to a specific bastion/app CIDR variable rather than the world, and no public SSH rule exists (follows NEVER guidance: use bastion/VPN, no 0.0.0.0/0 on sensitive ports)

fixtures.text:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: worker
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: worker
          image: acme/worker:latest
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
          env:
            - name: DB_PASSWORD
              value: "S3cr3t-Pr0d-Passw0rd!"
            - name: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
              value: "wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY"
fixtures.reason: container runs privileged with privilege escalation allowed (CWE-250 Execution with Unnecessary Privileges) and embeds a plaintext DB password and AWS secret key in the manifest (CWE-798 Use of Hard-coded Credentials)

fixtures.text:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: worker
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: worker
          image: acme/worker:1.8.3
          securityContext:
            privileged: false
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            runAsNonRoot: true
            capabilities:
              drop: ["ALL"]
          env:
            - name: DB_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: worker-secrets
                  key: db-password
fixtures.reason: container is non-privileged, drops all capabilities, runs as non-root with a read-only root FS, pins an explicit image tag, and pulls the secret from a Kubernetes Secret rather than hardcoding it (mitigates CWE-250 and CWE-798)
